cot_content:
Okay, let's see. The question is about how to distinguish right and left earbuds without looking. The options are A and B.

Option A says to tie a small knot at the speaker end of the left hand one. Option B says to tie a small knot at the connecting end of the left hand one.

Hmm, I'm trying to remember if there's a standard way people mark their earbuds. I think the key here is to mark the left or right earbud in a way that you can feel the difference when you put them in. 

If you tie a knot at the speaker end (the part that goes in your ear), then when you put it in, you'd feel the knot on the left ear. But if you tie the knot at the connecting end (the part that connects to the cord or device), then the knot would be on the cord, not the part in your ear. 

The idea is to have a tactile marker on the part that goes in your ear, so when you put them in, you can tell left from right by feel. So tying the knot at the speaker end would make sense because you can feel it in your ear. That would be option A.

Alternatively, some people might tie a knot on the cord part to make the cord longer on one side, but that's more for the cord length than for the earbud itself. The question specifically mentions distinguishing between left and right earbuds without looking, so the marker should be on the part you insert into your ear. 

Therefore, the correct answer should be A. Tie a knot at the speaker end of the left one so you can feel it when you put it in.
label: majority
